hints.text: Mostly **Gen4 pickup-truck coverage** — usually blurred, but sometimes coloured car parts or a **snorkel** show
hints.uniq_note: Only Kenya and Mongolia have a Gen4 snorkel.

shared_with.note: The only other Gen4 snorkel country.

hints.text: Road lines: **outer always solid white**, inner **yellow** (solid or dashed)
Poles concrete or wood w/ an **L-shaped crossbar**

shared_with.note: A similar pole exists in Ghana.

hints.text: A **grey Toyota SUV follows** the Street View car on much of the coverage
Plates: **long white front / square YELLOW rear**
**Safaricom** mobile-network shops — unique to Kenya, in almost every city
Directional signs **green w/ white border + text**, road numbers white/yellow; other signs mostly red-white

shared_with.note: Vans there too — but the vehicle-wrap design clearly differs.

hints.text: **Eucalyptus** very common (long clumped leaves, smooth bark) — supporting clue
Gen3: visible **roof rack + snorkel** (some Gen3 shows no car at all)
Pole markings w/ a **skull + danger message**
**County names** appear on signs/billboards/posters → learn the counties; **PO BOX postcodes**: 'PO BOX XXX-YYYZZ' — the number after the hyphen = area code, city name often follows

Coverage outside the SW mostly on main roads; **east + north = extremely flat, SW = higher**; climate zones humid → very arid (map); far west (around Kisumu) = the farm belt: grassy hills + eucalyptus; Kitui = semi-arid, light-orange sandy dirt; NW (A1/Sibiloi) = tan soil + sparse low trees + dirty exposed car

**Coast**: coconut palms (+ rare Taita-Taveta); white flowers en masse → east of Chyulu Hills / Tsavo / A109 south of Nairobi; endless **stick fences** → around Narok; **red brick** → west + east of Nairobi; mud-and-stick houses → west + coast; light bricks w/ excess mortar → coast

**Road spotlights**: B9 (NE) = unpaved bright red-orange dirt; A2 Isiolo↔Marsabit = plants + big ranges (huge range west in Namunyak); A2 north of Marsabit = flatter, barer; A3 north of Garissa = light soil + green trees; western C34 = flat E-W, sugar cane + a long range north; B8 north of Kibaoni = succulents

**Nairobi** = the only skyscraper city, ringed by poor settlements; **Mt Kilimanjaro** visible to the south from Oloitoktok & the border area; Homa Bay = protruding hill east; **Wajir** = blinding white dirt roads + extremely red roadside soil

bullets: Mara Triangle: white car (also other parks), flat/hilly, no big mountains, some Gen4
Amboseli: grey car w/ small snorkel — flat tan grass, dried lake beds, Kilimanjaro base south
Aberdare: grey-brown car w/ large snorkel; rubber bands on the roof rack on the main park road
Kitale↔Mt Elgon: same car but ALWAYS w/ rubber bands; redder dirt + greener
Kerio↔Lodwar: same car, but sandy dry barren landscape (some carless Gen3)
